what: Created ChangelogSection.tsx — a Client Component that allows users to select and configure which git branch to use for changelog generation.
why: The settings page needed an interactive component for branch selection. Implemented as a Client Component to handle dynamic branch fetching, user selection, and API calls independently.
technical_details: Component fetches available branches from /api/projects/[id]/branches, displays them in a select dropdown, and saves selection via PATCH to /api/projects/[id]. Handles loading, error, and saved states with proper UX feedback. Includes fallback for previously selected branches that may have been deleted.
side_effects: New Client Component dependency on useState, useEffect. New API calls to fetch branches and update project. Loading skeleton provided during branch fetch. Error messages shown to user on network or validation issues.

summary: Update Settings Page to Include ChangelogSection
what: Updated ProjectSettingsPage Server Component to fetch and pass changelogBranch from project, then render ChangelogSection in the settings layout between RepoSection and ApiKeysSection.
why: The settings page is the primary interface for project configuration. ChangelogSection needs to be integrated there with proper data flow from Server Component to Client Component.
technical_details: Added changelogBranch field to db.project.findUnique select. Server Component passes projectId, currentBranch (changelogBranch), and defaultBranch to ChangelogSection. This separates data fetching (server) from interactivity (client) per Next.js App Router patterns.
side_effects: ProjectSettingsPage now fetches one additional field from database. Slight increase in query size but no performance impact. ChangelogSection now visible and functional in the settings UI.
